heroHeadline: Day 59: Fractura Total Day 11 — Mexico Pledges National Guard Surge at Tourist Sites; ACLED Webinar in 24 Hours
heroSubtitle: Day 59 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). The CJNG fractura total — the declared internal war between the El Jardinero/El Sapo faction and O3/Los Cuinis (declared April 11) — enters Day 11 in sustained buildup with no direct inter-faction combat confirmed in the Jalisco heartland. On April 22, Security Secretary Omar García Harfuch announced an immediate National Guard surge at all major archaeological sites and tourist destinations nationwide, following the April 20 Teotihuacán pyramid shooting (non-CJNG; Canadian tourist killed, 13 injured by lone gunman Julio César Jasso). President Sheinbaum reiterated Mexico's World Cup 2026 security commitment: the government's 100,000-person deployment remains in place across Mexico City, Guadalajara, and Monterrey. ACLED's first major post-Mencho succession analysis webinar — 'After El Mencho: CJNG Succession Dynamics and the Future of Security in Mexico,' featuring InSight Crime experts Chris Dalby, Victoria Dittmar, and Sandra Pellegrini — is scheduled for April 23, 12:00 PM Mexico City time (24 hours from now). The fractura total's El Jardinero/El Sapo faction continues territorial positioning in northern Jalisco and the Zacatecas border zone (Monstruos armored vehicles near Colotlán, April 18); O3 retains Grupo Élite command and Los Cuinis financial infrastructure. Operativo Enjambre cumulative total: 80+ arrests. FIFA World Cup 2026: 50 days to the opening match (June 11, Mexico City). Guadalajara's Akron Stadium hosts four group-stage matches.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, U.S. Treasury (OFAC), InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, NPR, Wall Street Journal, El País, Washington Post, Al Jazeera, South China Morning Post, CGTN, France 24, Latin Times, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, WOLA, Milenio, Excélsior, Proceso, El Financiero, El Imparcial, Borderland Beat, RED Michoacán, Semanario ZETA, La Voz de la Frontera, TV Azteca Baja California, Time, CNN, ACLED, OCCRP, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: April 22, 2026.
